topic.what: Content-encoding watermarks combining cryptographic digital signatures with deep neural networks for image authentication
topic.how: Uses VQ-VAE to compress images into latent representations which are EdDSA-signed and embedded as watermarks; verifies integrity by comparing received image latent with signed encoding
topic.why_matters: Provides verifiable source attribution and integrity validation against AI-generated forgeries while remaining imperceptible and robust to image transformations

characteristics.what_it_measures: Ability to detect tampering attempts on signed images
characteristics.evidence: Section 1: 'We demonstrate that DeepSignature reliably identifies significant forgery attempts—achieving near 100% in our experiments'
characteristics.context: Experimental results on CLIC 2022 and CLIC 2024 test sets with 768x768 images

characteristics.what_it_measures: Effective payload bits per megapixel at JPEG quality 50
characteristics.evidence: Section 2.3 comparing HiDDeN and similar methods achieving 30-4000 bits per megapixel
characteristics.context: Range depends on message length and bit error rate requirements

characteristics.what_itasures: Cryptographic signature size using EdDSA Curve25519
characteristics.evidence: Section 3.2: Ed25519ph provides 128-bit security with 512-bit signature vs RSA requiring 3072 bits
characteristics.context: EdDSA pre-hashing with SHA512 for signing latent representations

characteristics.what_it_measures: Spatial reduction from image to latent representation
characteristics.evidence: Section 3.1: VQ-VAE with global stride S reduces H' = H/S, W' = W/S with additional scaling factor s
characteristics.context: Configurable via stride and scaling factor for application-specific trade-offs

characteristics.what_it_measures: Crossover probability under noisy channel transformation
characteristics.evidence: Section 3.3: BSC model with error correction enabling recovery at p > 0
characteristics.context: BCH forward error correction applied to watermark bits

not_for: real-time embedded systems with limited compute

novelty: First content-encoding watermarks combining cryptographic signatures with neural networks
Latent-space verification approach for tampering detection and localization
Application-specific trade-off mechanisms between imperceptibility robustness and verifiability
End-to-end system hiding compressed and signed image in itself
open_problems: Enhanced robustness against sophisticated adversarial attacks
Improved trade-offs between capacity imperceptibility and robustness
Seamless integration with existing media provenance standards like C2PA
Recovery quality optimization for self-recovery watermarking
